title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: US Stocks Decline Amid Inflation, Yield Concerns, and Tech Pullback - May 19, 2026

items.headline: US Equities Retreat as Inflation Fears and Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Investor Sentiment
items.summary: U.S. equities extended losses on Tuesday, pulling back from recent record highs as concerns over persistent inflation, rising bond yields, and geopolitical uncertainties, particularly the Iran conflict, rattled investor confidence. Technology stocks, which had driven much of the market's earlier rally, experienced a significant cool-down. While some global markets saw mixed movements, Wall Street largely trended downward throughout the day, with major indices closing in negative territory. Traders are also looking ahead to Nvidia's critical earnings report due tomorrow.

items.headline: Marathon Petroleum Surges in Premarket
items.summary: Shares of Marathon Petroleum ($MPC) were notably higher in premarket trading.

items.headline: Fox Corporation Rises in Early Trading
items.summary: Fox Corporation ($FOX) saw a strong gain in pre-market hours.

items.headline: Verisk Analytics Posts Solid Premarket Gains
items.summary: Verisk Analytics ($VRSK) climbed significantly during premarket activity.

items.headline: PulteGroup Plunges in Premarket
items.summary: Homebuilder PulteGroup ($PHM) experienced a sharp decline in premarket trading.

items.headline: Bank of Nova Scotia Falls in Early Trading
items.summary: The Bank of Nova Scotia ($BNS) saw a significant drop in pre-market hours.

items.headline: Akamai Technologies Declines on Convertible Note Offering
items.summary: Akamai Technologies ($AKAM) fell after announcing plans to offer $2.6 billion in convertible senior notes, intended to fund expansion and share repurchases.

items.headline: Technology and Semiconductor Stocks Lead Market Decline; Energy Shows Resilience
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly semiconductor stocks, faced significant pressure today, contributing heavily to the overall market's downturn. This comes after an extended period of strong gains fueled by AI optimism, with some analysts noting that valuations may have become stretched. The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index has notably fallen over 7% in the last three trading sessions. Conversely, the Energy sector demonstrated resilience, posting gains, with Information Technology and Consumer Staples also showing positive monthly trends amidst inflation concerns.

items.headline: Producer Prices Rise 6.0% Annually in April; Fed Signals Patience on Rate Cuts
items.summary: The Producer Price Index (PPI) for final demand climbed 6.0% year-over-year in April 2026, driven significantly by a 22.7% increase in energy prices. This continued inflationary pressure is influencing market sentiment, with analysts suggesting the Federal Reserve may delay interest rate cuts until the second half of 2027. Incoming Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, set to be sworn in on Friday, faces a challenging economic landscape.

items.headline: Treasury Yields Climb Higher Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty and Inflation Worries
items.summary: Bond markets continued to exert pressure on stocks, with the yield on the benchmark 10-year Treasury rising to 4.67% and the 30-year U.S. Treasury yield reaching 5.185%, its highest since 2007. This rise in yields reflects ongoing concerns about high inflation and the broader economic impact of the lingering Iran conflict, despite a temporary pause in planned U.S. military strikes. Oil prices, though easing slightly, remained elevated above $110 per barrel.

items.headline: Home Depot Reports Mixed Q1 Results; Nvidia Earnings Anticipated
items.summary: Home Depot ($HD) announced first-quarter fiscal 2026 sales of $41.8 billion, a 4.8% increase, with comparable sales up 0.6%. However, the stock declined after sales growth missed some analyst estimates, despite diluted EPS of $3.30. All eyes are now on chipmaker Nvidia ($NVDA), whose highly anticipated quarterly earnings report is scheduled for Wednesday, expected to be a key indicator for the broader technology sector. Toll Brothers ($TOL) is also slated to report earnings after market close today.
